pavan.title: Project Pavan
pavan.tagline: Adventure-puzzle vertical slice with crisp 3Cs, scalable gameplay systems, and readable AI encounters.
pavan.summary: Project Pavan is a mythology-inspired action adventure initiative under Fluke Games, focused on strong traversal, gameplay clarity, and scalable systems that contributors can build on.
highlights: Built as both a game project and a studio-scale learning platform
Combines player-facing systems with contributor-friendly architecture
Emphasizes strong gameplay readability and reusable systems

aiContext.summary: Project Pavan is a systems-first action adventure prototype under Fluke Games that blends gameplay experimentation with production-style team structure.
aiContext.myContribution: I lead the technical direction, define the gameplay and AI architecture, and structure the project so contributors can build against stable systems.
interestingProblems: scaling an indie project with AAA-style structure
keeping systems reusable for contributors
balancing ambition with vertical-slice execution

aiContext.summary: Prototype phase focused on movement foundations and first-pass enemy behaviour.
Alpha emphasized encounter structure, AI query systems, and early performance discipline.
Beta focused on tuning and validating the play experience rather than just building systems.
The slice phase is about turning the prototype into something presentation-ready and stable.

codename_interviewprepapp.title: Codename InterviewPrepApp
codename_interviewprepapp.tagline: In-development interview preparation platform exploring adaptive practice flows, structured learning paths, and AI-guided feedback.
codename_interviewprepapp.summary: Codename InterviewPrepApp is an early-stage product initiative focused on helping users prepare for interviews through structured practice, guided question flows, personalized feedback, and scalable learning workflows. The project is currently in the conception and proof-of-concept phase, with emphasis on validating product direction, system architecture, and AI-assisted coaching loops.
highlights: Early-stage product exploration for structured interview preparation
Combines learning design, product thinking, and AI-assisted feedback
Focused on validating user value before full-scale buildout

aiContext.summary: Codename InterviewPrepApp is an early product exploration around interview preparation, adaptive learning, and AI-guided practice experiences.
aiContext.myContribution: I am defining the product direction, validating core user journeys, shaping the architecture, and building proofs of concept for feedback and preparation workflows.
interestingProblems: making feedback useful without being overly generic
balancing structured preparation with flexibility
validating product value before deeper engineering investment

aiContext.summary: Conceptualization focused on clarifying the user problem, identifying the most valuable preparation flows, and defining the product thesis.
POC phase is validating whether guided practice, automated feedback, and lightweight analytics can work together in a meaningful way.
Architecture draft phase is beginning to formalize how the product could scale from experiments into a maintainable application.

crazy_cops_v2.title: Crazy Cops Version 2
crazy_cops_v2.tagline: Cross-platform multiplayer demolition game focused on arcade chaos, game feel, and networked systems.
crazy_cops_v2.summary: Crazy Cops V2 was a cross-platform multiplayer demolition and racing game built in Unity. The project combined fast arcade gameplay with networked multiplayer, weapon systems, and high-energy player interactions. I worked on gameplay programming, mode logic, and the systems that supported the overall feel and responsiveness of the experience.
highlights: Focused on fast, readable arcade feel

aiContext.summary: Crazy Cops V2 was an arcade multiplayer game combining racing, combat, and networked gameplay systems.
aiContext.myContribution: I built gameplay systems, mode logic, and player-facing mechanics for a high-energy multiplayer experience.
interestingProblems: keeping the experience readable in multiplayer chaos
balancing multiple game modes with shared systems

images.headline: Arcade Multiplayer Gameplay
images.description: The game emphasized high-energy player interaction and accessible competitive chaos.

images.headline: Unity Gameplay Programming
images.description: The systems were built with a focus on responsiveness, readability, and rapid iteration.

images.headline: Distinct Game Modes
images.description: Different modes introduced varied pacing while keeping the core interaction loop consistent.

images.headline: Vehicle Combat and Action
images.description: The gameplay blended destruction, movement, and timing-heavy player interaction.

images.headline: Competitive Play
images.description: The project explored how arcade feel could coexist with structured competitive modes.

aiContext.summary: Prototype validated the core arcade driving loop and multiplayer feasibility.
Production phase expanded the project from a testbed into a complete arcade multiplayer experience.
Polish focused on responsiveness, readability, and final gameplay tuning for release.

beat_slayer_vr.title: Beat Slayer VR
beat_slayer_vr.tagline: Virtual reality FPS arcade prototype focused on comfort-aware locomotion and multiplayer design.
beat_slayer_vr.summary: Beat Slayer VR was a multiplayer VR arcade shooter prototype built around the challenge of balancing freedom of movement with player comfort. The project explored alternative locomotion approaches that reduced nausea while preserving agency and gameplay intensity. I worked on gameplay systems, movement design, interaction logic, and prototype systems that supported an accessible but expressive VR experience.

aiContext.summary: Beat Slayer VR explored comfort-first locomotion and multiplayer-ready VR gameplay design.
aiContext.myContribution: I worked on VR gameplay systems, interaction design, movement architecture, and multiplayer prototype direction.
interestingProblems: balancing control freedom with motion comfort
designing VR interactions that remain intuitive under pressure
planning multiplayer synchronisation for immersive interactions

images.headline: Comfort-Oriented VR Design
images.description: The project investigated how to preserve control without forcing uncomfortable locomotion.

images.headline: Controller-Split Movement Logic
images.description: Movement responsibilities were distributed in a way that supported fluid direction changes.

images.headline: Reduced Nausea Through Interaction Design
images.description: The core design challenge was building a playable VR system that stayed comfortable over time.

images.headline: Gameplay-Driven VR Systems
images.description: Every movement choice was evaluated from both a comfort and gameplay readability perspective.

aiContext.summary: Prototype established the comfort-first movement foundation and core interaction loop.
Feature completion connected locomotion, combat, and networking into a cohesive prototype.
Final phase emphasized comfort validation, consistency, and demo readiness.

survival_rpg_game_setup.title: Survival RPG Game Setup
survival_rpg_game_setup.tagline: RPG systems prototype built around character progression, inventory, combat, and gameplay structure.
survival_rpg_game_setup.summary: This RPG setup project explored foundational RPG mechanics in Unreal Engine using both Blueprints and C++. The goal was to build an example project containing common RPG systems such as inventory, equipment, skills, progression, and combat. It helped demonstrate systems thinking and gameplay architecture through a cohesive mechanics-driven prototype.

aiContext.summary: This project was a systems-focused RPG prototype showcasing progression, inventory, and combat mechanics.
aiContext.myContribution: I built and integrated foundational RPG systems in Unreal using a mix of Blueprints and C++.
interestingProblems: structuring gameplay systems so they remain extensible
connecting progression systems to combat and equipment cleanly

images.headline: Core RPG Systems
images.description: The project served as a practical exploration of reusable RPG mechanics.

images.headline: Gameplay Progression Design
images.description: Systems such as leveling, equipment, and skills were connected into one coherent framework.

aiContext.summary: Foundation phase established the reusable architecture for core RPG systems.
Integration phase connected the major systems into a coherent RPG framework.
Showcase phase refined the project into a clean portfolio-ready systems sample.

reinforcement_learning_ai_ml_agents.title: Reinforcement Learning AI ML Agents
reinforcement_learning_ai_ml_agents.tagline: Applied reinforcement learning project exploring self-learning agents for game-like environments.
reinforcement_learning_ai_ml_agents.summary: This project explored reinforcement learning for enemy AI and agent behaviour in interactive environments. Using PPO and ML-Agents style approaches, the work focused on training agents to navigate, collect rewards, and make behaviour decisions in a game setting. It combined machine learning research with game AI thinking, showing how learned behaviours could augment traditional systems.
highlights: Explored RL for game-like enemy behaviour
Built agent training scenarios with rewards and penalties
Connected ML thinking with practical AI systems design

aiContext.summary: This project explored reinforcement learning as a way to build adaptive, self-improving behaviour in game environments.
aiContext.myContribution: I designed the learning setup, agent objectives, and simulation scenarios for behaviour training using PPO-style reinforcement learning.
interestingProblems: defining useful reward structures
making learned behaviour stable enough to evaluate
connecting research ideas to practical game AI applications

images.headline: RL for Game AI
images.description: The work examined how machine learning could support more adaptive enemy behaviour.

images.headline: Beyond Traditional AI
images.description: The project explored where hand-authored systems struggle and where learned behaviour can add value.

images.headline: Intelligent Agent Behaviour
images.description: The focus was on creating agents that improve through interaction rather than explicit scripting alone.

aiContext.summary: Initial phase established the training environment and baseline learning loop.
Main research phase focused on stabilizing training and evaluating learned behaviour quality.
Final phase packaged the work into a clear, demonstrable research outcome.

blender_fps_shooting_game.title: Blender FPS Shooting Game
blender_fps_shooting_game.tagline: Early FPS mechanics experiment covering scripting, animation, modelling, and interaction systems.
blender_fps_shooting_game.summary: This project was an early exploration of FPS game mechanics using Blender's game development workflow. It covered multiple disciplines, including modelling, rigging, texturing, and scripting, while focusing on the implementation of core first-person mechanics such as movement, control, and inventory logic.
highlights: Combined art pipeline work with gameplay scripting

aiContext.summary: This project was an early first-person gameplay prototype combining art pipeline work and gameplay system implementation.
aiContext.myContribution: I handled multiple areas including gameplay logic, modelling support, rigging understanding, and prototype system construction.
interestingProblems: building gameplay systems while also learning content pipeline fundamentals
keeping mechanics coherent in a small experimental setup

images.headline: FPS Mechanics Experiment
images.description: The project explored the fundamentals of movement, interaction, and weapon handling.

images.headline: Gameplay + Content Pipeline Learning
images.description: It combined systems work with modelling, rigging, and animation understanding.

aiContext.summary: Core prototype established the first-person interaction loop and control feel.
Integration phase connected gameplay logic with content pipeline learnings.
Final phase packaged the prototype as a compact but complete portfolio sample.

augmented_reality_foundation_core.title: Augmented Reality Foundation/Core
augmented_reality_foundation_core.tagline: Foundation-level AR experimentation across multi-platform Unity workflows.
augmented_reality_foundation_core.summary: This project explored Unity AR Foundation as a platform for multi-device augmented reality development. It focused on understanding the system architecture, platform abstraction, and how AR interaction patterns can be structured across supported devices.
highlights: Focused on core cross-platform AR concepts

aiContext.summary: This project explored AR Foundation as a cross-platform system for building augmented reality experiences.
aiContext.myContribution: I worked on understanding and prototyping the AR systems flow needed for multi-platform augmented reality development.
interestingProblems: building AR features in a platform-agnostic way
understanding how abstraction layers affect gameplay systems

aiContext.summary: Initial phase focused on grasping the architectural foundation of AR Foundation workflows.
Interaction pass turned the technical setup into usable AR gameplay experiments.
Final phase positioned the project as a concise AR reference and exploration piece.

vista_mart.title: Vista-Mart
vista_mart.tagline: AngularJS and Spring Boot e-commerce platform built for student-focused delivery workflows.
vista_mart.summary: Vista-Mart was an e-commerce web application designed for students living in hostels around Chandigarh. The platform supported ordering groceries and dairy products and included common commerce flows such as account management, cart, checkout, and merchant-side inventory support. It was an early full-stack project that combined frontend UI, backend services, and business workflows.
highlights: Built an end-to-end web commerce workflow
Handled both customer-facing and merchant-facing systems

aiContext.summary: Vista-Mart was a student-focused e-commerce platform built as a full-stack web application.
aiContext.myContribution: I worked on the full web flow including frontend UI, backend services, and core commerce functionality.
interestingProblems: connecting user flows cleanly to backend logic
supporting multiple commerce use cases in one application

images.headline: Target Audience
images.description: The application was designed around a very practical delivery use case for students.

images.headline: Technology Stack
images.description: It combined AngularJS on the frontend with Spring-based backend services.

aiContext.summary: MVP established the essential commerce flow for browsing and ordering.
Expansion phase added the operational pieces needed for a more complete commerce system.
Release phase focused on making the application coherent, usable, and portfolio-ready.
